summary: Officially documented user environment variable for opting the .NET CLI out of telemetry. Good for privacy-focused or quiet developer workstations, but it removes default telemetry signals from the .NET SDK and CLI.

setting.name: .NET CLI Telemetry Opt-Out
setting.casual_explanation: The .NET SDK and CLI can send telemetry about tool usage. This tweak sets the current user's opt-out environment variable so new .NET CLI sessions stop sending that telemetry.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want quieter developer tooling, less usage reporting from the .NET SDK, or a privacy-focused developer profile.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Opting out reduces telemetry from the .NET CLI, but it also removes usage and crash signals that Microsoft uses for diagnostics and product improvement.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT and says values such as 1 and true opt the .NET CLI out of telemetry. Microsoft also documents that persistent user environment variables are stored for the current user under HKCU\Environment.

allowed_values.label: Default telemetry behavior
allowed_values.meaning: The .NET CLI uses its normal telemetry behavior because no persistent opt-out variable is set for the current user.

allowed_values.label: .NET CLI telemetry opted out
allowed_values.meaning: The .NET CLI telemetry is opted out for the current user.

allowed_values.label: .NET CLI telemetry opted out
allowed_values.meaning: The .NET CLI telemetry is opted out for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT = 1 as a persistent current-user environment variable.

windows_defaults.label: Windows default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current-user profiles that have not explicitly set the .NET CLI telemetry opt-out variable

states.rationale: Leave the .NET CLI opt-out variable unset.

windows_defaults.notes: Without this environment variable, the .NET CLI keeps its normal telemetry baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Developer machines that want the default .NET telemetry baseline
avoid_for: Managed developer workstations that should opt out

states.rationale: Leave the .NET CLI opt-out variable unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the .NET SDK and CLI to keep their default telemetry behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to minimize usage reporting from .NET developer tools.
tradeoffs: No user-level privacy opt-out is enforced for the .NET CLI.

recommended_profiles.label: CLI privacy
avoid_for: Users who want to leave .NET CLI telemetry enabled

states.rationale: Set the documented .NET CLI opt-out variable.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the .NET CLI to stop sending its default telemetry for the current user.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to leave the tool on its default telemetry baseline for diagnostics or feedback.
tradeoffs: .NET CLI telemetry is opted out for the current user.
New terminal windows or new shell sessions may be needed before all tools pick up the updated environment.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: .NET SDK and .NET CLI telemetry
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT and says setting it to 1 or true opts the .NET CLI out of telemetry.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Win32_Environment class
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents persistent user environment variables and their Windows storage model, which maps to HKCU\Environment for the current user.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT = 1 under HKCU\Environment for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft directly documents the opt-out environment variable, and the app writes a supported opt-out value in the current user's persistent environment store.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: The .NET SDK telemetry feature is enabled by default... To opt out of the telemetry feature, set the DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT environment variable to 1 or true.; Win32_Environment: Querying this class returns environment variables found in HKEY_USERS\<user>\Environment.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft directly documents DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT as the opt-out environment variable, and Win32_Environment documents that persistent user environment variables are stored under HKEY_USERS\<user>\Environment. The app writes the current user's variable under HKCU\Environment, which is the current-user view of that store.
